unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: In this unit you will learn about the people in your neighbourhood and the different jobs they do. You will meet community helpers like the police officer, the firefighter, and the bus driver, and find out how neighbours help each other. You will learn to ask questions with “What” and “Who”, count people, and say what people are doing right now. By the end, you will be ready to describe a job and be a good neighbour, just like the children in this unit's story.
unit.learningPath:
Watch the teacher audiovisual lecture, then learn a few vocabulary words each day.
Complete the six grammar practices on What/Who and actions happening now.
Listen to the four readings, including The Helpers of Warta Street, and answer the comprehension questions.
Complete all six speaking, six writing and six activity practices.
Join three live teacher sessions each week for two weeks.
Finish the ten-question quiz and student self-assessment.

visual.alt: Children meeting helpful workers in their neighbourhood

readings.title: Firefighters and Their Equipment
readings.passageScript:
Read this short text:
Firefighters have important jobs. They put out fires. They rescue people who are inside burning buildings. Firefighters wear special clothes that keep them safe. They wear heavy boots on their feet. They wear helmets to keep their heads safe. A firefighter wears a mask on the face to breathe fresh air, and heavy gloves to touch hot things.

readings.title: A Poem: “My Neighbourhood”
readings.passageScript:
Come and meet the people in my neighbourhood.
There are neighbours helping neighbours in my neighbourhood.
There are grandmas and grandpas,
Mums and dads, children too,
In my neighbourhood, the streets around my home.

readings.title: An Interview with a Firefighter
readings.passageScript:
Listen to Miss Isaac, a firefighter. (Ask your AI tutor to read it aloud, then read it yourself.)
“I am a firefighter. I work at the fire station. When there is a fire, I ride on the fire engine. I use water to fight the fire. I also visit schools and talk to children about fire safety.”
Complete this report about Miss Isaac:
Answers: 1. firefighter  2. fire station  3. rides  4. water  5. schools / fire safety

readings.title: The Helpers of Warta Street
readings.genre: A gentle story about neighbours, with lots of talking.
readings.theme: Good neighbours, community helpers, and kindness.
readings.setting: A busy little town in East Africa.
readings.passageScript:
Samira lived on Warta Street, near the market. She loved her street because everyone helped each other. The people who lived near her were her neighbours, and they were kind.
One hot morning, Samira sat by her window. She watched the busy street.
“Look, Hooyo,” she said to her mother. “The window cleaner is here!”
A tall man named Ishmael was cleaning the windows of the shop next door. He used a long pole to reach the high glass.
“Good morning, Samira!” Ishmael called. “What are you doing today?”
“I am watching all the helpers,” said Samira. “Who else works on our street?”
Ishmael smiled. “Many good people work here,” he said. “Look — here comes the bus driver.”
A big blue bus stopped at the corner. The bus driver, Selma, waved. She wore a neat uniform.
“Hello, Samira!” said Selma. “I am driving to the city. Do you want to count my passengers?”
Samira laughed and counted. “One, two, three… ten! There are ten people on your bus!”
Just then, a loud bell rang. Samira saw smoke behind the market.
“A fire!” shouted Ishmael. “Everyone, stay back!”
A red fire engine came fast down the street. Two firefighters jumped out. They wore helmets on their heads, heavy boots on their feet, and thick gloves on their hands. One firefighter wore a mask on her face to breathe.
“What are they doing?” asked Samira.
“They are putting out the fire,” said her mother. “And look — they are rescuing the baby goats!”
The firefighters used water to fight the fire. Soon the smoke was gone. Everyone on Warta Street cheered.
A police officer named Adam arrived on his bicycle. He kept the people safe.
“Please stand back, my friends,” said Adam kindly. “The market is safe now. Thank you for helping each other.”
That is when Samira saw old Sarah. Sarah sold dates and bread in the market. Her small shop was full of smoke, and she looked sad.
“Hooyo, we must help her,” said Samira.
So Samira and her mother ran to help. Ishmael brought water. Selma swept the floor. Adam carried the heavy boxes. Even the two firefighters helped clean the shop.
“Thank you, my good neighbours,” said Sarah, wiping her eyes. “You all have important jobs, but today you helped an old woman. That is the kindest job of all.”
Sarah gave everyone fresh dates. They were sweet and warm.
That night, Samira told her father about the day. “A window cleaner, a bus driver, firefighters, a police officer,” she said. “Everyone on Warta Street helps.”
“Yes,” said her father. “And so do you. A good neighbour is the best helper of all.”
Samira smiled and closed her eyes. Tomorrow she would sit by her window again — and maybe help a neighbour too.

comprehension.question: What do firefighters do? List two things.
comprehension.correctAnswer: They put out fires and rescue people from burning buildings.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: What special clothes do firefighters wear? List at least three.
comprehension.correctAnswer: Firefighters wear helmets, heavy boots, masks and heavy gloves.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: Why do firefighters wear masks? Why do they wear gloves?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Masks help firefighters breathe fresh air; gloves protect their hands from hot things.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: Draw a firefighter with all their special equipment.
comprehension.correctAnswer: Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

comprehension.question: Who lives in the neighbourhood in the poem?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Grandmas, grandpas, mums, dads and children live in the neighbourhood.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: What are the neighbours doing?
comprehension.correctAnswer: The neighbours are helping one another.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: Try to add a new line about your own neighbourhood. (You will write more later.)
comprehension.correctAnswer: Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

comprehension.question: Miss Isaac is a ______.
comprehension.correctAnswer: firefighter
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit for this answer or an equivalent response that communicates the same meaning.

comprehension.question: She works at the ______.
comprehension.correctAnswer: fire station
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit for this answer or an equivalent response that communicates the same meaning.

comprehension.question: When there is a fire, she ______ on the fire engine.
comprehension.correctAnswer: rides
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit for this answer or an equivalent response that communicates the same meaning.

comprehension.question: She uses ______ to fight the fire.
comprehension.correctAnswer: water
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit for this answer or an equivalent response that communicates the same meaning.

comprehension.question: She also visits ______ and talks to children about ______.
comprehension.correctAnswer: schools; fire safety
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit for this answer or an equivalent response that communicates the same meaning.

comprehension.question: On which street does Samira live? What is near it?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Samira lives on Warta Street, near the market.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: What is Ishmael's job? What does he use to clean the high windows?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Ishmael is a window cleaner. He uses a long pole to reach the high windows.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: How many people are on Selma's bus?
comprehension.correctAnswer: There are ten people on Selma's bus.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: What special clothes do the firefighters wear? Name three.
comprehension.correctAnswer: The firefighters wear helmets, heavy boots, thick gloves and a mask.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: How did the neighbours help old Sarah after the fire?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Ishmael brought water, Selma swept, Adam carried boxes, and the firefighters helped clean Sarah's shop.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: Why did Samira say helping her was “the kindest job of all”?
comprehension.correctAnswer: They chose to help her after finishing their own important jobs, showing kindness and teamwork.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: Who are the community helpers in this story? Which one would you like to be?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

comprehension.question: Samira's father said, “A good neighbour is the best helper of all.” What does this mean?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Anyone can notice a need and help kindly; helping is not limited to a person's paid job.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: How do you help your neighbours? How do they help you and your family?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

grammar.title: What and Who: Choose the Question Word
grammar.explanation: Use what to ask about a thing, action or job. Use who to ask about a person.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
What is Selma doing? She is driving the bus.
Who is driving the bus? Selma is driving the bus.
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: Who is the firefighter doing?
Fix: What is the firefighter doing?
grammar.memoryTip: Who asks for a person. What asks for information about a thing or action.
grammar.practice: Choose What or Who: ___ is the bus driver? | ___ is the doctor doing? | ___ is helping Samira? | ___ does a firefighter wear? | ___ is your teacher?
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

grammar.title: What and Who: Build the Question
grammar.explanation: Begin with What or Who, keep the words in question order, and finish with a question mark.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
Who helps sick people? A doctor helps sick people.
What is the farmer growing? The farmer is growing maize.
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: Who the bus drives?
Fix: Who drives the bus?
grammar.memoryTip: Question word first, question mark last.
grammar.practice: Build one What question and one Who question for each scene: a firefighter using a hose; a nurse helping a child; a farmer growing beans.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

grammar.title: Actions Now: Choose Is or Are
grammar.explanation: Use the present continuous for an action happening now. Use is with one person or thing and are with more than one.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
The driver is waving.
The firefighters are climbing.
We are helping our neighbours.
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: The firefighters is climbing.
Fix: The firefighters are climbing.
grammar.memoryTip: One is; more than one are.
grammar.practice: Complete with is or are: The officer ___ helping. | The children ___ counting. | Samira ___ watching. | We ___ learning. | The farmers ___ growing maize.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

grammar.title: Action Words: Add -ing
grammar.explanation: Add -ing to many action words to show that the action is happening now. Some words change before -ing is added.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
help + ing = helping
drive - e + ing = driving
run + n + ing = running
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: driveing.
Fix: driving. Drop the final e before adding -ing.
grammar.memoryTip: Look at the action word before you add -ing.
grammar.practice: Write the -ing form: help | teach | rescue | drive | clean | run.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

grammar.title: Actions Now: Build Complete Sentences
grammar.explanation: A complete present-continuous sentence needs a subject, is or are, and an action word ending in -ing.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
The window cleaner is cleaning the glass.
The neighbours are carrying boxes.
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: The nurse helping the child.
Fix: The nurse is helping the child.
grammar.practice: Put the words in order: is / Ishmael / cleaning / the window | are / the firefighters / water / using | helping / Samira and Hooyo / are / Samira.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

grammar.title: Neighbourhood Reporter
grammar.explanation: Use the present continuous to report what different people are doing in a busy neighbourhood scene.
grammar.ruleAndExamples: Selma is driving the bus. Two firefighters are putting out the fire. Adam is keeping people safe.
grammar.commonMistake: Check that is or are matches the person or people you name.
grammar.memoryTip: Look, name, choose is or are, then say the action.
grammar.practice: Describe six actions in the neighbourhood picture. Use at least three sentences with is and two sentences with are.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

speaking.title: Speaking 1 — Describe a Job
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines:
Choose a job. Get ready to say:
“A ______ (job) helps people by ______.”
“A ______ (job) wears ______.”
“A ______ (job) uses ______.”
Record yourself. Check: Did you name the job? Did you say what they do? Did you speak clearly?

speaking.title: Speaking 2 — Ask and Answer Questions
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines:
Look at a picture of people working. For each person, ask and answer:
“What is the ______ doing?”  →  “The ______ is ______.”
“Who is helping the ______?”  →  “______ is helping.”

speaking.title: Speaking Game — Who Am I?
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines:
Play with your AI tutor or your family:
Think of a job, but keep it secret. Act it out (put out a fire, drive a bus, clean a window).
The other person asks: “Are you a ______?”
Answer: “Yes, I am!” or “No, I am not.” Then swap turns.

speaking.title: Speaking 4 - Who and What Detective
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Look at a neighbourhood scene. Ask three Who questions and three What questions. Model: Who is driving the bus? Selma is driving. What is Ishmael doing? He is cleaning a window.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Answer my six neighbourhood questions, then tell me whether I used Who and What correctly.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - What Is Happening Now?
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Describe six actions in the picture using is or are and an -ing word. Model: The firefighter is carrying a hose. The neighbours are helping Samira.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Listen to my six action sentences. Check my use of is, are and -ing.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - Be a Good Neighbour
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Role-play noticing that a neighbour needs help. Ask what happened, offer help and respond politely. Model: What happened? Your box is heavy. I can help you carry it. Thank you, good neighbour!
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Role-play a neighbour who needs simple help, then give me feedback on my questions and kind response.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

writing.title: Writing 1 — Simple Sentences About Jobs
writing.promptAndInstructions:
Choose a job. Write three sentences about it — what the person does, what they wear, and how they help:
A firefighter puts out fires. A firefighter wears a helmet and boots. A firefighter helps people who are in danger.
writing.modelText: A firefighter helps people. A firefighter wears a helmet and uses a hose. This job keeps our neighbourhood safe.
writing.successCriteria: I answered the prompt; used Unit vocabulary; wrote complete sentences; used capital letters, spaces and end punctuation; reread my work.
writing.support: Use the sentence starter, word bank and model. Say each sentence aloud before writing.
writing.extension: Add two precise details, a joining word and one sentence explaining why.

writing.title: Writing 2 — Your Own Neighbourhood Poem
writing.promptAndInstructions:
Write your own version of “My Neighbourhood”. Change some of the words to fit your own home:
Come and meet the people in my neighbourhood. / There are ______ helping ______ in my neighbourhood. / There are ______ and ______, / ______ and ______, ______ too, / In my neighbourhood, the streets around my home.
Then draw a picture to go with your poem.
writing.modelText:
Come and meet the helpers on my street.
Drivers and teachers are people I meet.
Neighbours help when work must be done.
Working together can help everyone.
writing.sentenceStarter: Come and meet the helpers on my street.
writing.successCriteria: I answered the prompt; used Unit vocabulary; wrote complete sentences; used capital letters, spaces and end punctuation; reread my work.
writing.support: Use the sentence starter, word bank and model. Say each sentence aloud before writing.
writing.extension: Add two precise details, a joining word and one sentence explaining why.

writing.title: Story response: The Helpers of Warta Street
writing.promptAndInstructions:
Write three sentences about a helper on your own street. Start with: “On my street, the ______ helps by ______.”
Draw the helpers of Warta Street. Write one sentence under each person about what they are doing. (Example: “The bus driver is driving.”)
writing.modelText: The people on Warta Street helped one another. The firefighters stopped the fire and the neighbours shared what they had. A good neighbour notices when someone needs help.
writing.sentenceStarter: The people on Warta Street helped one another.
writing.successCriteria: I answered the prompt; used Unit vocabulary; wrote complete sentences; used capital letters, spaces and end punctuation; reread my work.
writing.support: Use the sentence starter, word bank and model. Say each sentence aloud before writing.
writing.extension: Add two precise details, a joining word and one sentence explaining why.

writing.title: Writing 4 - A Community Helper Card
writing.promptAndInstructions: Create a fact card about one community helper. Name the job, explain two duties, and name two clothes or tools used for the job.
writing.modelText: A firefighter helps people. A firefighter puts out fires and rescues people. Firefighters wear helmets and boots. They use a hose and ladder.
writing.successCriteria: I named the job; explained two duties; named two clothes or tools; used complete sentences and punctuation
writing.support: Use the jobs-and-equipment word bank and the model card.
writing.extension: Add a sentence explaining why the job is important.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

writing.title: Writing 5 - Warta Street News Report
writing.promptAndInstructions: Write a short report about the fire on Warta Street. Tell where it happened, who came to help, what people did and how the event ended.
writing.modelText: A fire started near the market on Warta Street. Firefighters came in a red fire engine. They put out the fire and rescued the goats. The neighbours helped Samira clean her shop. Everyone was safe in the end.
writing.successCriteria: I explained where, who, what and the ending; used story facts; put events in order; checked capitals and punctuation
writing.support: Use the story, the sequence words first, next and finally, and the model report.
writing.extension: Add one quotation from a character.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

writing.title: Writing 6 - My Good-Neighbour Promise
writing.promptAndInstructions: Write six sentences about how you can be a good neighbour at home, at school or on your street. Include one action you can do this week.
writing.modelText: I can greet my neighbours kindly. I can carry a bag for an older person. I can keep our shared space clean. I can listen when someone needs help. This week, I will help sweep near our door. Good neighbours work together.
writing.sentenceStarter: I can be a good neighbour by
writing.successCriteria: I wrote six relevant promises; included one action for this week; used clear verbs; checked complete sentences
writing.support: Use I can and I will sentence starters.
writing.extension: Explain how one promise will help another person.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

activities.title: Activity 1 — Match Jobs and Tools
activities.instructionsAndItems:
Match each job to the tools it uses.
Firefighter — ______
Police officer — ______
Doctor — ______
Teacher — ______
Farmer — ______
Choose from: hose and ladder · badge and whistle · stethoscope and medicine · books and board · tractor and seeds
activities.answerSummary: 1. hose and ladder  2. badge and whistle  3. stethoscope and medicine  4. books and board  5. tractor and seeds

activities.title: Activity 2 — Fill in the Blanks (Job Descriptions)
activities.instructionsAndItems:
A firefighter puts out ______ and rescues ______.
A police officer keeps people ______ and directs ______.
A doctor helps people who are ______ and gives them ______.
A teacher ______ students and helps them ______.
A farmer grows ______ and raises ______.
activities.answerSummary: 1. fires / people  2. safe / traffic  3. sick / medicine  4. teaches / learn  5. crops / animals

activities.title: Activity 3 — True or False (Jobs and Actions)
activities.instructionsAndItems:
Write True or False. If it is false, write the correct answer.
Police officers help keep people safe.
Doctors grow crops and vegetables.
Bus drivers take people from place to place.
Teachers put out fires in buildings.
activities.answerSummary: 1. True  2. False (doctors help sick people; farmers grow crops)  3. True  4. False (teachers teach students; firefighters put out fires)

activities.title: Activity 4 — Fill in the Blanks (Firefighter Equipment)
activities.instructionsAndItems:
Firefighters wear ______ on their heads to protect them.
They wear heavy ______ on their feet.
They wear ______ on their hands to protect them from heat.
A firefighter wears a ______ on the face to help breathe.

activities.title: Activity 5 - Who Uses It?
activities.instructionsAndItems: Sort each item under the correct helper: hose | helmet | stethoscope | medicine | books | board | badge | whistle | seeds | tractor.
activities.answerSummary: Firefighter: hose, helmet; doctor: stethoscope, medicine; teacher: books, board; police officer: badge, whistle; farmer: seeds, tractor.
activities.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

activities.title: Activity 6 - Neighbourhood Action Hunt
activities.instructionsAndItems: Find or imagine six people helping in a neighbourhood. For each person, record the job and one action happening now using is or are + verb-ing.
activities.answerSummary: Answers vary; require six sensible job-and-action pairs with correct is/are + verb-ing sentences.
activities.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

assignments.title: Good Neighbours and Jobs learning portfolio
assignments.instructions: Complete 'Writing 1 — Simple Sentences About Jobs' and record 'Speaking 1 — Describe a Job'. Submit both pieces after checking the writing and speaking rubrics.
assignments.sourceFile: Derived from unit writing and speaking tasks

quizzes.question: What does 'neighbour' mean?
quizzes.options: A person who lives near you. | A person whose job is to clean windows. | A person who keeps people safe and helps them follow the law. | A person who finds out news and writes stories about it.
quizzes.correctAnswer: A person who lives near you.
quizzes.explanation: My neighbour Nadia grows flowers in her garden.

quizzes.question: Which word means: A person whose job is to clean windows.
quizzes.options: window cleaner | neighbour | police officer | reporter
quizzes.explanation: The window cleaner uses a long pole to reach the high windows.

quizzes.question: What type of word is 'police officer'?
quizzes.explanation: The police officer helped the children cross the busy road.

quizzes.question: The ____ asked questions about our market day.
quizzes.options: reporter | neighbour | window cleaner | police officer
quizzes.explanation: The reporter asked questions about our market day.

quizzes.question: Which spelling is correct?
quizzes.explanation: The bus driver takes people from our town to the city.

quizzes.question: Who helps people who are sick?
quizzes.options: A doctor | A farmer | A bus driver | A reporter
quizzes.explanation: A doctor helps people who are sick.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

quizzes.question: Which question asks about an action?
quizzes.options: What is the firefighter doing? | Who is the firefighter? | Who is your neighbour? | Who drives the bus?
quizzes.explanation: Use What to ask about an action.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

quizzes.question: The firefighters ___ climbing the ladder.
quizzes.explanation: Firefighters means more than one person, so use are.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

quizzes.question: Which is the correct -ing form of drive?
quizzes.explanation: Drop the final e in drive before adding -ing.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

quizzes.question: Which clothes protect a firefighter's head and feet?
quizzes.options: A helmet and boots | A mask and gloves | A uniform and badge | A coat and scarf
quizzes.explanation: A helmet protects the head and strong boots protect the feet.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

liveSessions.title: Meet the neighbourhood helpers
liveSessions.beforeSession: Watch the teacher vocabulary lecture and learn the People in Your Neighbourhood group.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min welcome; 10 min picture talk; 10 min vocabulary retrieval; 15 min describe-a-job speaking; 5 min reflection
liveSessions.afterSession: Complete Speaking 1 and add five helpers to My Word Book.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

liveSessions.title: Firefighters, equipment and safety
liveSessions.beforeSession: Listen to Firefighters and Their Equipment and bring one safety question.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min retrieval; 10 min shared reading; 10 min equipment sorting; 15 min comprehension discussion; 5 min safety exit ticket
liveSessions.afterSession: Complete Activity 4 and improve two reading answers.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

liveSessions.title: Ask with What and Who
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min warm-up; 10 min What/Who sort; 10 min teacher modelling; 15 min question detective speaking; 5 min feedback
liveSessions.afterSession: Record Speaking 4 and correct one question.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

liveSessions.title: Actions happening now
liveSessions.beforeSession: Complete Grammar Practices 3-6 and practise the action words.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min action mime; 10 min is/are check; 10 min build -ing words; 15 min neighbourhood reporting; 5 min review
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

liveSessions.title: The Helpers of Warta Street
liveSessions.beforeSession: Listen to the story and choose one helper you admire.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min prediction; 10 min echo reading; 10 min event sequencing; 15 min Warta Street news writing; 5 min peer feedback
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

liveSessions.title: Good-neighbour showcase and review
liveSessions.beforeSession: Complete the ten-question quiz and bring one portfolio piece.
liveSessions.agenda: 10 min retrieval game; 10 min quiz corrections; 10 min role-plays; 10 min portfolio shares; 5 min self-assessment
liveSessions.afterSession: Complete Writing 6 and save the final self-assessment.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

teacherNotes.note:
Lesson: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you read the poem with me and explain it?” “Can you play the firefighter interview again?” “What is fire safety?”
Lesson: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you help me practise describing jobs?” “Can you play ‘Who Am I?’ with me?” “How do I ask a ‘What’ question and a ‘Who’ question?”
Lesson: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you check if my sentences are correct?” “Can you help me make my poem about my neighbourhood?”
Story: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you read the story with me and explain any hard words?” “Can you check my sentences about the helpers on my street?” “Can you ask me questions about the story to see if I understood?”
Grammar: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you check my grammar answers?” “When do I use ‘What’ and when do I use ‘Who’?” “Why do we say ‘she is’ but ‘they are’?”
Vocabulary: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “What other community helpers can you tell me about?” “How do I describe what a ______ does?” “Can you help me learn more words about people in my community?”
Vocabulary: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “What special equipment does a ______ use?” “Why do firefighters need to wear special clothes?” “Can you help me learn more words about job equipment?”
Vocabulary: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “What does a ______ do all day?” “Can you tell me about more jobs in a town?”
Vocabulary: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you help me use these action words in sentences?” “How do I add ‘-ing’ to an action word?”

Term placement approved. Outcome mappings, comprehension guidance, vocabulary enrichment, assessments, live sessions, rubrics, writing specifications and answer-key coverage completed in the approved reference model.
teacherNotes.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1 migration

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: 1. hose and ladder  2. badge and whistle  3. stethoscope and medicine  4. books and board  5. tractor and seeds
1. fires / people  2. safe / traffic  3. sick / medicine  4. teaches / learn  5. crops / animals
1. True  2. False (doctors help sick people; farmers grow crops)  3. True  4. False (teachers teach students; firefighters put out fires)
1. helmets  2. boots  3. gloves  4. mask
1. Who   2. What   3. Who   4. What   5. Who

The police officer is helping the children.
The firefighters are climbing the ladder.
My neighbour is cleaning the windows.
We are learning about jobs.
The farmer is growing maize.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: A firefighter wears a mask on the face to breathe fresh air, and heavy gloves to touch hot things. Firefighters wear special clothes that keep them safe.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: A firefighter wears a mask on the face to breathe fresh air, and heavy gloves to touch hot things. Firefighters wear special clothes that keep them safe.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: Firefighters wear special clothes that keep them safe. They wear heavy boots on their feet. They wear helmets to keep their heads safe. A firefighter wears a mask on the face to breathe fresh air, and heavy gloves to touch hot things.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: Firefighters wear special clothes that keep them safe. They wear heavy boots on their feet. They wear helmets to keep their heads safe. A firefighter wears a mask on the face to breathe fresh air, and heavy gloves to touch hot things.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: Firefighters wear special clothes that keep them safe. A firefighter wears a mask on the face to breathe fresh air, and heavy gloves to touch hot things.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: Firefighters wear special clothes that keep them safe. A firefighter wears a mask on the face to breathe fresh air, and heavy gloves to touch hot things.

Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
Marking guidance: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: Come and meet the people in my neighbourhood.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: Come and meet the people in my neighbourhood.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: There are neighbours helping neighbours in my neighbourhood.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: There are neighbours helping neighbours in my neighbourhood.

schools; fire safety

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: Samira lived on Warta Street, near the market. She loved her street because everyone helped each other.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: Samira lived on Warta Street, near the market. She loved her street because everyone helped each other.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: “The window cleaner is here!” A tall man named Ishmael was cleaning the windows of the shop next door. He used a long pole to reach the high glass.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: “The window cleaner is here!” A tall man named Ishmael was cleaning the windows of the shop next door. He used a long pole to reach the high glass.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: “Many good people work here,” he said.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: “Many good people work here,” he said.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: “One, two, three… ten!
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: “One, two, three… ten!

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: Thank you for helping each other.” That is when Samira saw old Sarah.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: Thank you for helping each other.” That is when Samira saw old Sarah.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: That is the kindest job of all.” Sarah gave everyone fresh dates.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: That is the kindest job of all.” Sarah gave everyone fresh dates.

Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
Marking guidance: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: A good neighbour is the best helper of all.” Samira smiled and closed her eyes.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: A good neighbour is the best helper of all.” Samira smiled and closed her eyes.

Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
Marking guidance: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.
Correct answer: A person who lives near you.. My neighbour Nadia grows flowers in her garden.
Correct answer: window cleaner. The window cleaner uses a long pole to reach the high windows.
Correct answer: Noun. The police officer helped the children cross the busy road.
Correct answer: reporter. The reporter asked questions about our market day.
Correct answer: bus driver. The bus driver takes people from our town to the city.
Assess the writing with rub-writing-v1 and the recording with rub-speaking-v1. Award up to 16 marks for each artifact.
